top_signals.title: Iran to define Strait of Hormuz administration with Oman and Gulf states, senior U.S. officials say
top_signals.summary: Senior U.S. officials disclosed the contents of the memorandum of understanding with Iran. The MOU pledges 60 days of toll-free shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Hostile states behind three-quarters of attacks on Britain's critical infrastructure, cyber chief warns
top_signals.summary: NCSC CEO Richard Horne warned that “kinetic targeting in any conflict tomorrow will be based on intelligence gathered today” and that nation-state adversaries were “prepositioning” throughout British critical infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Kazakhstan Bets $10 Billion on AI With Nvidia-Backed Data Center Valley
top_signals.summary: The Kazakh government has inked a $10 billion deal with US chip-making giant Nvidia and an Armenian-American cloud company to develop a 'Data Center Valley' in the country’s northern Pavlodar region. The deal is seen as anchoring a broader effort to turn Kazakhstan into a global digital hub. Kazakhstan recently adopted a development strategy called “Digital Qazaqstan“ to guide the country’s transformation into a cognitive economy through 2029.  “Digital Qazaqstan represents a fundamental transition of Kazakhstan…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs lost $111 million combined as rate-cut hopes died
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Woodside sees LNG, oil sales rising 50% as investments from US to Australia pay off
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Japan saw record 3,623 people encounter mountaineering accidents in 2025
top_signals.summary: Overall, accidents mainly occurred due to people getting lost, or falling or slipping on slopes, though bear attacks accounted for 27 cases — tripling from the previous year.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

FirstFT: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum controversy in China - Financial Times

top_signals.title: Job vacancies fall again in unemployment risk
top_signals.summary: Job vacancies available across the UK dropped again, official data revealed, posing a further risk for Britons out of work.  The Office for National Statistics said the number of job vacancies fell by 19,000 between March and May. The unemployment rate dropped to 4.9 per cent per cent compared to five per cent in data [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The Invisible Energy Crisis Threatening to Derail the AI Boom
top_signals.summary: Every AI boom forecast being published right now — every bull case, Big Tech earnings call, and valuation model — seems to be making the same assumption. The electricity will be there to power it when they need it. It won’t. Bitzero (NASDAQ: AIBZ) spent the last four years betting against that assumption. The company locked in more than a gigawatt of low-cost power across Norway, Finland, and North Dakota, well before the rest of the industry started fighting over every available megawatt. The company is already cash flow positive,…
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
